commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex cost scanning so unresolved fork parent baselines are untrusted, skips cumulative `total_token_usage` rows when the parent log is missing, still counts incremental `last_token_usage`, updates the parser hash, and adds regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main collapses a missing fork parent to `nil` and then counts child cumulative totals as raw usage. I did not execute the synthetic test because the review must keep the checkout read-only.

target.title: Avoid Codex fork overcount when parent logs are missing
target.body:
Fixes #1138.

This treats unresolved fork parent baselines as untrusted for Codex cost scanning. When the parent session log is missing, cumulative child `total_token_usage` snapshots are no longer counted as fresh usage. Incremental `last_token_usage` rows still count.

Validation:
- `swift test --filter CostUsageScannerBreakdownTests`
- `swift test --filter CostUsageFetcherTests`
- `make check`
target.author: jskoiz
